worth_it_explanation: Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) reaches supply through three distinct paths. Yahoo owned-and-operated (O&O) premium properties, private marketplace (PMP) deals on third-party exchanges, and open exchange buying across multiple exchanges each carry a different quality profile and fee structure. A campaign whose supply mix has never been reviewed is likely spending across all three paths in proportions set by defaults rather than deliberate choices. These checks require nothing beyond the Yahoo DSP user interface and the line-level targeting screen, and the fixes are configuration changes that take effect on the next auction cycle.
intro: In Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform), each line targets one of two mutually exclusive inventory paths: exchanges or deals. A line targeting exchanges reaches open exchange supply across whichever Yahoo or third-party exchanges are selected, plus Yahoo O&O (owned-and-operated) properties when the Yahoo exchange is included. A line targeting deals reaches only the PMP deals registered to that line. These checks confirm that the supply mix reflects deliberate choices, that Yahoo O&O premium supply is used where its authenticated, logged-in user context fits the campaign, and that open exchange buying includes the fraud and brand safety controls public inventory requires.

audit_checks.title: Yahoo O&O premium supply is not targeted on campaigns where its authenticated context would improve performance
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Yahoo O&O properties such as Yahoo Mail, Yahoo Finance, Yahoo News, and Yahoo Sports carry logged-in, authenticated first-party audiences that are unavailable on the open exchange. A campaign that buys only open exchange supply misses that authenticated context entirely, even when the campaign's audience targeting and campaign goal would benefit from it.

educate.base:
Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) gives buyers access to Yahoo's owned-and-operated (O&O) properties alongside open exchange and private marketplace (PMP) supply. Yahoo O&O includes Yahoo Mail, Yahoo Finance, Yahoo News, Yahoo Sports, and AOL properties. These environments carry logged-in, authenticated users whose identity is tied to Yahoo's first-party data graph rather than inferred from third-party cookies or probabilistic matching.

When a line in Yahoo DSP targets the Yahoo exchange, it reaches Yahoo O&O supply as part of that exchange's inventory pool. A line that targets only open exchange without including the Yahoo exchange, or that has blocked Yahoo O&O placements through an exclusion list, is buying entirely on supply where user identity is third-party-derived or cookie-based.

This matters most for two campaign types. Brand awareness campaigns that want to demonstrate adjacency to trusted editorial content benefit from Yahoo O&O's premium editorial properties. Retargeting and audience campaigns that rely on stable user identification benefit from authenticated first-party data, which is more durable than third-party cookie matching as cookie deprecation continues to reduce match rates on open exchange.

A few signs suggest Yahoo O&O supply is not in the active supply mix:
  • Lines targeting exchanges do not include the Yahoo exchange in their targeting selection
  • An app or site exclusion list blocks yahoo.com, finance.yahoo.com, or mail.yahoo.com domains
  • All active lines target deals only, with no exchange-targeting lines that would include Yahoo O&O supply
You may want to confirm with your Yahoo DSP account team whether O&O inventory is accessible under your current contract before adjusting targeting, as access may depend on the buyer's relationship with Yahoo.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an ecommerce advertiser running prospecting campaigns, Yahoo Finance and Yahoo Shopping environments reach users in a commercial mindset. You may want to consider whether adding the Yahoo exchange to lines that currently target only third-party exchanges would give access to O&O inventory that aligns better with purchase-intent audiences.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, authenticated users on Yahoo O&O properties have a known profile that reduces the risk of form submissions from non-human or misattributed traffic. You may want to review whether lines focused on upper-funnel lead nurturing include the Yahoo exchange in their targeting selection.

learn_more.source_title: Yahoo Buying Guide: Target exchanges and deals in Yahoo DSP

educate.learn_more_note: Yahoo DSP's Help Center documentation on inventory sources and O&O supply targeting requires a Yahoo DSP account login to access. Contact your Yahoo DSP account team or consult the in-platform Help section for O&O supply configuration details.

investigate.intro: Review the exchange and deal targeting on each active line and confirm whether Yahoo O&O supply is reachable given the current targeting selection.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Open the advertiser, navigate to Campaigns, and select a campaign to review. On the Lines list, open each active line.
Within the line, go to the Targeting page and locate the Exchanges and Deals section. Note whether the line targets Exchanges or Deals.
If the line targets Exchanges, review the list of selected exchanges. Note whether the Yahoo exchange is included in the targeting selection or excluded.
If the line targets Deals only, note that it cannot simultaneously target exchanges. Deals-only lines do not reach Yahoo O&O open supply unless a specific O&O PMP deal is registered and targeted.
Open any active app or site exclusion list assigned to the line and confirm whether yahoo.com, mail.yahoo.com, finance.yahoo.com, or similar Yahoo O&O domains appear in the exclusion list.
Repeat for the remaining active lines in the campaign and record which lines include or exclude Yahoo O&O supply.

evaluate.logic: For each active line, record whether the targeting includes the Yahoo exchange (and therefore Yahoo O&O supply), targets deals only, or explicitly excludes Yahoo O&O domains. A line whose campaign goal would benefit from authenticated first-party context and that includes the Yahoo exchange with no O&O domain exclusions passes. A line on a brand or audience campaign that excludes Yahoo O&O supply with no documented rationale is a finding, recorded with the line name and the missing exchange or active exclusion.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record each active line's exchange or deal targeting selection and any active site exclusion list entries for Yahoo domains to complete the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that active lines in {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) account do not include the Yahoo exchange in their targeting selection, leaving Yahoo's owned-and-operated (O&O) premium properties out of the active supply mix. Yahoo O&O inventory on properties such as Yahoo Mail, Yahoo Finance, and Yahoo News carries authenticated, logged-in first-party audiences that are not available on the open exchange. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the exchange targeting on lines whose campaign goals would benefit from authenticated audience context and adding the Yahoo exchange where it is appropriate, confirming that no site exclusion lists block Yahoo O&O domains inadvertently.

audit_checks.title: Lines reach the same publishers through multiple redundant reseller paths with no supply path optimization applied
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When a Yahoo DSP line buys the same publisher's inventory through both direct-authorized and reseller paths, each intermediary in the reseller chain adds fees and reduces the share of the bid that reaches the publisher. Supply path optimization (SPO) reduces redundant reseller exposure by preferring shorter, authorized paths, but it requires an active configuration choice at the line level.

educate.base:
When a Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) line targets open exchange supply across multiple exchanges, it may reach the same publisher's inventory through more than one path simultaneously. A direct path means the exchange serving the bid is listed as a direct, authorized seller in the publisher's Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB) ads.txt file. A reseller path means one or more intermediaries stand between the exchange and the publisher, each adding a fee before the cleared bid reaches the publisher's ad server.

SPO is the practice of identifying and preferring the shortest, most authorized path to a publisher when multiple paths are available for the same impression. Without SPO, a DSP bids on the same impression opportunity across all available paths at once, and the winning path may be a longer reseller chain rather than the direct seller, even when both paths are available at similar clearing prices.

Yahoo DSP exposes inventory quality targeting controls at the line level that allow buyers to filter by ads.txt and app-ads.txt declaration status. Enabling the ads.txt declaration filter restricts buying to inventory where the selling exchange is listed as an authorized seller in the publisher's ads.txt file, which removes a category of unauthorized reseller paths. Without this filter, the line bids on inventory regardless of whether the selling exchange is authorized by the publisher.

A few signs suggest redundant supply paths are active without SPO controls:
  • Lines targeting multiple exchanges simultaneously with no ads.txt or app-ads.txt declaration filter enabled
  • Exchange-level delivery reporting shows a meaningful share of impressions on exchanges where the same publisher appears as a reseller rather than a direct seller
  • Lines have no app or site list that would restrict buying to publishers with confirmed direct exchange relationships
You may want to review the Yahoo DSP buying guide for the available ads.txt declaration targeting options before enabling the filter, as it may reduce delivery if a publisher's ads.txt file is incomplete or outdated.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an ecommerce advertiser, a high share of reseller-path impressions means a portion of the media budget pays intermediary fees rather than reaching the publisher. You may want to confirm whether the exchanges targeting the campaign's key audience segments carry strong direct-seller relationships for those publisher categories.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, reseller-path inventory is statistically more likely to include invalid traffic (IVT) because unauthorized resellers face fewer verification requirements than direct sellers. You may want to review whether enabling the ads.txt declaration filter reduces the IVT rate on lines where form submissions are the campaign goal.

learn_more.source_title: Yahoo Buying Guide: Ads.txt and App-ads.txt Declaration targeting

investigate.intro: Review whether active lines have the ads.txt declaration filter enabled and whether delivery is concentrated on exchanges where the key publishers appear as authorized direct sellers.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Open the advertiser, navigate to Campaigns, and select a campaign to review. On the Lines list, open each active line.
On the Targeting page, scroll to the Additional Targeting Features section. Find the 'Ads.txt and App-ads.txt Declaration' targeting option and note whether it is enabled or disabled.
Note the list of exchanges selected in the Exchanges and Deals section. Count how many exchanges are actively targeted simultaneously on lines with open exchange buying.

In the campaign reporting, run a delivery breakdown by exchange. Identify which exchanges account for the largest share of impressions. For the top exchanges by spend, note whether those exchanges are likely to carry direct-seller relationships with the publishers your campaign targets.

If a third-party verification vendor such as DoubleVerify or Integral Ad Science (IAS) is active, pull the supply chain report and review the share of impressions flagged as reseller-path or ads.txt-unauthorized.

evaluate.logic: For each active line with open exchange targeting, record whether the ads.txt declaration filter is enabled and whether delivery is spread across a large number of exchanges with no path-quality control. A line with the ads.txt declaration filter enabled, or with targeting restricted to a defined set of exchanges where direct-seller relationships are documented, passes. A line targeting multiple exchanges simultaneously with no ads.txt filter and no exchange-level delivery review on record is a finding, recorded with the line name and the number of exchanges targeted without path controls.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record each active line's ads.txt declaration targeting status and the number of simultaneously targeted exchanges to complete the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that active lines in {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) account target multiple exchanges simultaneously without the ads.txt declaration filter enabled, meaning the lines bid on supply regardless of whether the selling exchange is authorized by the publisher in their ads.txt file. This configuration allows bids to travel through reseller chains rather than direct publisher relationships, adding intermediary fees and reducing path transparency. {{voice_recommend}} enabling the ads.txt and app-ads.txt declaration filter on open exchange lines as a first step toward supply path optimization (SPO), and reviewing the exchange-level delivery breakdown to identify which exchanges account for the largest impression share so targeting can be narrowed to the exchanges with the strongest direct-seller relationships for the campaign's key publishers.

audit_checks.title: Open exchange lines run without bot avoidance or viewability quality controls enabled
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Open exchange supply in Yahoo DSP includes inventory across all exchanges the line targets, which spans a wide range of quality profiles. Without bot avoidance or viewability inventory quality controls, the line bids on every impression the targeted exchanges offer, including impressions with high invalid traffic (IVT) rates and low probability of a human user seeing the ad. Yahoo DSP exposes these controls at the line level, so the cost of not enabling them is paid on every impression the line buys.

educate.base:
Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) provides inventory quality targeting controls at the line level under the Additional Targeting Features section of the Targeting page. The Yahoo Buying Guide documents three categories of inventory quality controls available to buyers:

Bot avoidance blocks fraud and malware sites. When enabled, this filter removes inventory on domains and apps identified as serving significant volumes of non-human traffic or hosting malware. Without it, the line bids on those environments at the same rate as legitimate publisher supply.

Viewability targets highly viewable inventory. When enabled, this filter restricts the line to impressions Yahoo's measurement partners have classified as likely to be viewable by a real user. Without it, the line bids on impressions regardless of whether the ad placement is visible on screen, which means a share of the budget pays for impressions that a human user never had the opportunity to see.

Contextual targeting allows buyers to target or block specific content categories. This control is relevant both for brand safety (blocking content categories the brand should not appear next to) and for relevance (targeting content categories that match the campaign's audience intent).

These controls are independent of each other and can be applied in combination. A line with bot avoidance enabled but viewability not enabled avoids known fraud environments but still buys non-viewable impressions on legitimate sites. A line with neither control enabled is bidding on open exchange supply with no quality filter beyond whatever the selected exchanges apply at their own thresholds.

A few signs indicate inventory quality controls have not been reviewed on open exchange lines:
  • The Inventory Quality Targeting section on the line's Targeting page shows no active selections under Bot avoidance, Viewability, or Contextuals
  • Post-campaign reporting shows a share of impressions with zero interaction or engagement rate that is inconsistent with the campaign's audience size
  • Lines have run for multiple weeks on open exchange with no exchange-level quality review
You may want to confirm which inventory quality controls are appropriate for the campaign's goal before enabling them since viewability and bot avoidance filters can reduce available inventory and may require a bid adjustment to maintain delivery volume.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an ecommerce advertiser, an impression on a bot-served or non-viewable placement does not generate a product view or contribute to purchase intent regardless of how precisely the audience was defined. You may want to confirm that bot avoidance is enabled on all open exchange lines as a baseline quality floor before reviewing other targeting settings.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, form submission rate depends on a real user seeing the ad and acting on it. You may want to review whether enabling viewability targeting on open exchange lines, in addition to bot avoidance, improves cost-per-lead by reducing spend on impressions that a real user was unlikely to see.

learn_more.source_title: Yahoo Buying Guide: Inventory Quality Targeting including bot avoidance and viewability

educate.learn_more_note: Yahoo DSP's Help Center documentation on inventory quality targeting controls and their effect on delivery and pricing requires a Yahoo DSP account login to access. Consult the in-platform Help section or your Yahoo DSP account team for configuration guidance.

investigate.intro: Review the inventory quality targeting settings on each active open exchange line and confirm whether bot avoidance and viewability controls are enabled.

yahoo_dsp_ui.label: Yahoo DSP UI
steps: Open the advertiser, navigate to Campaigns, and select a campaign to review. On the Lines list, open each active line that targets exchanges rather than deals.
On the Targeting page, scroll to the Additional Targeting Features section. Locate the 'Inventory Quality Targeting' controls.
Note whether 'Bot avoidance' is enabled. If it is not shown as active, the line is bidding on exchange supply without blocking known fraud and malware environments.
Note whether 'Viewability' is enabled. If it is not active, the line bids on impressions regardless of their probability of being viewable by a real user.
Note whether any Contextuals targeting is active. Record which content categories are targeted or blocked, or confirm that no contextual filter is applied.
Repeat for all active open exchange lines and record which lines have zero inventory quality controls enabled.

evaluate.logic: For each active open exchange line, record the status of bot avoidance, viewability targeting, and contextual targeting. A line with bot avoidance enabled and a viewability control appropriate for the campaign format passes. A line with no inventory quality controls active and no documented rationale for the omission is a finding, recorded with the line name and the missing controls. A line targeting deals only is out of scope for this check, as deal targeting restricts to pre-negotiated supply rather than the full open exchange pool.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record each active open exchange line's inventory quality targeting selections, including bot avoidance, viewability, and contextual settings, to complete the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that open exchange lines in {{site_name}}'s Yahoo DSP (demand-side platform) account are running without the inventory quality targeting controls the platform provides at the line level. Bot avoidance and viewability targeting are not enabled, meaning the lines bid on open exchange supply without filtering out known fraud and malware environments or restricting to impressions a real user was likely to see. {{voice_recommend}} enabling bot avoidance as a baseline quality floor on all open exchange lines, evaluating whether viewability targeting is appropriate for the campaign's format and goal, and reviewing the contextual targeting options to confirm the active supply mix aligns with the brand's content adjacency standards.
